description: Japanese era from October 1868 to July 1912
capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: Higher education institution in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Chiba University (千葉大学, Chiba Daigaku) is a national university with campuses located in Chiba Prefecture, a region noted for its industrial, intellectual and international achievements.

Yamaoka Tesshū (山岡 鉄舟; June 10, 1836 – July 19, 1888), also known as Ono Tetsutarō or Yamaoka Tetsutarō, was a famous samurai of the Bakumatsu period, who played an important role in the Meiji Restoration.

aliases: The Pilgrim’s Progress from This World to That Which is to Come
description: 1678 Christian allegory written by John Bunyan
description_wiki: The Pilgrim's Progress from This World, to That Which Is to Come is a 1678 Christian allegory written by the English nonconformist preacher John Bunyan.

description: collection of sacred books in Judaism and Christianity
description_wiki: The Bible is a collection of religious texts that are central to Christianity and Judaism, and esteemed in other Abrahamic religions such as Islam.
Kisarazu (木更津市, Kisarazu-shi) is a city located in Chiba Prefecture, Japan.

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

description: group of Eastern Catholic Churches following the Byzantine (Greek) liturgy
description_wiki: The Greek-Catholic Churches, which fall within the "Eastern Catholic Churches" or "Catholic Churches of Eastern rite", constitute the corpus of churches in communion with the Bishop of Rome and observing, by long tradition, the Byzantine Rite.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: national university located in Kyoto, Japan
description_wiki: Kyoto University (京都大学, Kyōto daigaku), or KyotoU (京大, Kyōdai), is a national research university in Kyoto, Japan.

description: college and university system in the U.S. state of Hawaiʻi

description_wiki: Viscount Ishii Kikujirō (石井 菊次郎; April 24, 1866 – May 25, 1945), was a Japanese diplomat and cabinet minister in Meiji, Taishō and early Shōwa period Japan.
Tasuku Harada(原田 助) (December 20, 1863 – February 21, 1940) was a Japanese pastor and the president of Doshisha University from 1907 to 1919.
Waseda University (Japanese: 早稲田大学), abbreviated as Waseda (早稲田) or Sōdai (早大), is a private research university in Shinjuku, Tokyo.

surface_forms: Heartmountain years old at that time, V she looked olderVto me probably be- n Hrat Camp
description_wiki: The Heart Mountain War Relocation Center, named after nearby Heart Mountain and located midway between the northwest Wyoming towns of Cody and Powell, was one of ten concentration camps used for the internment of Japanese Americans evicted during World War II from their local communities (including their homes, businesses, and college residencies) in the West Coast Exclusion Zone by the executive order of President Franklin Roosevelt (after the bombing of Pearl Harbor in December 1941, upon the recommendation of Lieutenant General John L. DeWitt).

description: ethnic group; Americans of Japanese ancestry
description_wiki: Japanese Americans (Japanese: 日系アメリカ人) are Americans of Japanese ancestry.
